paragraphs.body: Why is it that every top story and news article is just some new feud with some new female celebrity? These photos are few of the many articles like this. For years the media has been obsessed with pitting women against each other. Whether it’s singers, actresses, or influencers it seems that whenever a woman gains fame, she’s pulled into some kind of “feud” with another woman. From well publicized feuds between Selena Gomez, Olivia Beiber, and Kyile Jenner, to the ongoing feud surrounding Miley Cyrus and Nicki Manaj, or the constant new drama going on with Taylor Swift and various other female celebrities. It feels like this narrative of constant female rivalries is the norm. This pattern is not just in the entertainment industry but extends to many parts of society. As these so-called “rivalries” are blown way out of proportion and are extremely exaggerated the underlying message becomes coherently clear: success can only be claimed by one woman at a time. This narrative is not only damaging–it's a lie.

paragraphs.body: In a world that tries so hard to pit women against each other, I’ve begun to discover the importance of having close female friends. Some of my strongest bonds are the women close to me. I cherish these bonds I have formed in the girl groups I’ve been lucky to be a part of, especially the deep connection I share with my sister. Through late-night talks, spontaneous road trips, and countless moments of laughter, my sister and I have crafted a world that feels uniquely ours. We have jokes that only we would understand. Every time my sister says, "I've got you ugly," I know for sure that she has my back, no matter what the situation is. That phrase reflects the bond we share, a bond built on loyalty, understanding, and the belief that we are stronger together. Or times when I am sad and just need someone I always know that my sister will always be there for me like a built-in best friend. It’s in these small moments that I find comfort, knowing that I can always rely on my sister to stand by me, cheer me on, and lift me up.

annotations.comment: Nicely written paragraph! This is lovely :)

paragraphs.body: Just like the bond I share with my sister, I have also created similar connections with my best friends. Over time, these relationships have developed into something uniquely special, creating this bond that we call “Girl Code”. Girl Code is a way we communicate our thoughts and feelings. It's also a highlight of how close our bond is as women and friends. This is what makes us feel safe and reassured in a world that too frequently tends to be hostile or indifferent. Girl Code connects us as women. Our girl code language was born through the numerous nightlong conversations, inside jokes, and shared experiences we have. An example of this language is, when we say "bing-challing," we are really referencing our group as a whole and the unbreakable bond in our friendships. It is symbolic of loyalty and an understanding that the girls got your back at any moment in your life. Another phrase we use is "burn the bread" which literally means to pass on the latest news or drama, but it also opens a free space where we can just share feelings and experiences without judgment. Another phrase that summarizes our way of empowering one another is "bing always wins." It's not just a slogan, but it is how we uplift one another and celebrate each other's successes. If one achieves something or successfully conquers a problem, we say it to boost morale and hype each other up. This Girl Code is so important to me in so many ways. In a world that often tries to tear women apart, having such a bond reminds us that we are not isolated; we are powerful when we stand together.

paragraphs.body: In a world where women are often portrayed as being in competition with each other, these types of friendships are revolutionary. We need one another—not just for support, but to uplift each other and create a safe environment where we can be our authentic selves without fear of being judged. Society often tells us that there’s only space for a few women at the top, that we must compete for recognition. This awful narrative creates unnecessary division amongst women. It's a lie designed to keep us from realizing our true power as women when we work together and have eachothers backs.

“Angelia and Jennifer beef over the years” “Taylor Swift and every women she has feuded with in Hollywood” “Olivia bashes Selena over tik tok” “Christina and Britney continue to fight for fame” Headlines like these continue to be flooding all social media and news platforms. When in reality it's just the media trying to promote the same tiring and toxic idea that women can’t get along and that there's only room for one woman at the top but I think it's time we rewrite these narratives and change these headlines. Women are not enemies: we are allies.

annotations.text: Women are not enemies: we are allies.

paragraphs.body: Women are so powerful together that society feels threatened by our strength and tries to tear us apart. It's time to embrace the power of women standing together, supporting one another, and celebrating each other's victories because the truth is: women are stronger together. When we stop feeding into and believing these false rivalries between strong women, and instead start breaking down these rumors and start lifting each other up, that's when we will change the world. As we continue to challenge the media’s harmful narratives, we can cultivate a culture of collaboration rather than competition. The world may try to tear women apart but the strength and bonds we share as women are truly unbreakable. Appreciate those women around you. Support them, love them, and cheer them on because we all need it. There is a special strength that comes from women supporting women and it's the kind of strength that can change the world.
